context_note: Nectar alkaloid is explicitly described as a defensive compound; captive bumblebee behavior tended toward reduced visitation, with magnitude dependent on alternative-flower context.
Transgenic lines altered release of floral volatile components; some components prevented florivory and reduced damage. Abstract has no pollinator outcome.

taxon: Cinnyris talatala; Pycnonotus tricolor; Zosterops virens
context_note: Nicotine in artificial nectar is described as an anti-herbivore defensive secondary metabolite; increased nicotine reduced feeding frequency and duration in sunbirds, with species and sucrose-context variation.
Field and laboratory nectar-alkaloid additions reduced bumblebee visits and time per flower only at concentrations far above natural nectar; natural-range treatments had no demonstrated cost.
